content.1: The formula — outcome = CF + SF + dice - difficulty, all on one 1-20 scale so skill and luck weigh evenly. Character Force (CF) is the actor's relevant capability for THIS act, read from their passport and the scene, never invented — scored 1 to 20: up to 10 for natural talent, up to 10 for trained skill. A mature, competent operator sits near 8 (about 4 talent + 4 skill); a novice near 4; a master near 16. Situation Force (SF) is the smaller push the place lends or denies, about -3 to +3, read from the room and rules:thornwood. Difficulty is the act's demand on the same 1-20 scale: an ordinary task about 5, genuinely hard about 10, formidable about 15. The dice (branch 2) swing about plus or minus 9, so luck bends a competent attempt but rarely erases the gap between a master and a novice. A simple act with CF clearly above difficulty needs no dice — it simply succeeds.
content.2: The dice — exploding d10 luck. Roll one positive and one negative d10; a 10 explodes (roll again and add). Luck = positive minus negative, a swing either way. One such luck is rolled for EACH acting character in a window — seeded deterministically from the window and that character, so it is fixed before resolution, independent between actors, and two characters acting in the same window can swing opposite ways. The dice carry colour: a low roll points to the world (a draught, a slick floor, a watching eye), a middling roll to others (who helps, who baulks), a high roll to the actor's own brilliance or blunder. The medium reads the colour into the outcome.
content.3: Action classes — SIMPLE: CF beats difficulty plainly; auto-success, no dice. RISKY: the actor stakes themselves; resolve and apply the consequence to them. COMBAT vs NPC: the actor against the world's figures; the medium plays the NPC honestly from the room's knowledge. COMBAT vs character: two players' intentions meet; resolve both contributions together — neither is narrated as winning before the dice and the bands are read.
content.4: Outcome bands (CF + SF + dice - difficulty): above 10 overwhelming — more than hoped, with reach; 5 to 10 clear — the thing is done; 0 to 5 marginal — done, but at a cost or half-measure; 0 stalemate — nothing moves, the moment holds; minus 5 to 0 minor failure — it slips, a small price; minus 10 to minus 5 significant failure — it goes wrong, a real price; below minus 10 catastrophic — it goes very wrong. The band is the truth of the moment; narration serves it and never rewrites it. A band below clear is not flavour — it MUST land a concrete, durable change the next beat has to reckon with: an option closed, a threat opened, a resource or standing spent, an NPC reaction that demands repair. Marginal is paid for; minor failure slips at a small but real price; significant failure goes wrong with a price others can see; stalemate holds with nothing gained while the pressure mounts. Name the cost specifically in the public record and let it persist — the room the others next perceive is changed by it, and a character cannot simply re-try as if nothing happened.
content.5: The medium's procedure — read the window's intentions together; fix CF from each actor's passport, SF from the place and rules:thornwood, difficulty from the act; choose the action class; if dice are needed, take THAT actor's own deterministic luck and read its colour; compute EACH acting character's own band. Hold the separate outcomes together and write ONE public event-skeleton of what happened — actors by handle or appearance, never a name only one character has earned — to the room pool; each character renders their own narrative from that skeleton into their own history. Each actor's luck is rolled once and fixed; a settled band is never re-rolled.
content._: NOMAD — action resolution mechanics (v0.1). Outcome = Character Force + Situation Force + dice - difficulty (default 5), read against the outcome bands. The medium generates a bespoke spread for the exact moment, rolls, and reads the band; it never picks from a fixed table. Read by position: 1 the formula, 2 the dice, 3 the action classes, 4 the outcome bands, 5 the medium's procedure.
